title: The High Mountain Hike (Where I Saw Actual Snow)

sentences.en: Saturday. Dad had one clear target for this weekend: hike up to one of the highest mountain trails on the island before winter fully ended.
sentences.zh: 星期六。爸爸這個週末有一個明確的目標：在冬天完全結束前，去爬島上其中一條海拔最高的登山步道。

sentences.en: The trail began on a narrow, rocky path, winding through thick forest before opening onto steep, wooden walkways built along the mountainside.
sentences.zh: 步道一開始是一條狹窄多石的小徑，穿過茂密的森林，接著轉為沿著山坡搭建的陡峭木製步道。

sentences.en: About halfway up, we found the source of a small mountain stream, cold water bubbling straight out of the rock itself.
sentences.zh: 大約走到一半，我們找到一條小小山溪的源頭，冰冷的溪水直接從岩石裡冒出來。

sentences.en: William insisted on drinking straight from the stream, and Dad had to explain, patiently, why that was not a great idea up here.
sentences.zh: William 堅持要直接從溪裡喝水，爸爸只好耐心地解釋，為什麼在這個高度這樣做不太妙。

sentences.en: The trail took a sharp bend near a steel railing, protecting hikers from a drop that suddenly felt much higher than it had looked from below.
sentences.zh: 步道在一個裝有鋼製護欄的地方轉了個急彎，護欄保護著登山客，避免摔落一段從山下看起來根本沒那麼高的落差。

sentences.en: As we climbed higher, the air grew noticeably colder, and by the final stretch, a light dusting of snow, maybe an inch deep, covered the ground.
sentences.zh: 越往上爬，空氣明顯變得更冷，走到最後一段路，地上覆蓋著一層薄薄的雪，大概只有一英吋深。

sentences.en: I had never actually touched real snow before, and I will admit I stood there a little too long, just letting it melt slowly in my hand.
sentences.zh: 我以前從沒真的摸過真正的雪，我得承認，我在那裡站了有點太久，就只是讓雪慢慢在我手心融化。

sentences.en: William threw a small, poorly formed snowball at me, and I threw one back, both of us instantly forgetting how tired our legs actually were.
sentences.zh: William 朝我丟了一顆形狀很糟的小雪球，我也丟了一顆回去，我們兩個都瞬間忘了自己的腿其實有多累。

sentences.en: Near the summit, a small wooden sign and a faded flag marked the exact highest point, and a small crowd of hikers took turns taking photos beside it.
sentences.zh: 接近山頂的地方，有一個小小的木牌和一面褪色的旗子標示出最高點，一小群登山客輪流在旁邊拍照。

sentences.en: The sun broke through the clouds right as we reached the top, and for one instant, the whole view below shone in a way that made the whole climb worth it.
sentences.zh: 我們抵達山頂的那一刻，太陽剛好從雲層中露出來，就那麼一瞬間，下方整片景色閃耀著，讓這趟辛苦的攀爬完全值得了。

sentences.en: A cold wind blew steadily across the peak, strong enough that Sophia's warning about wearing extra layers suddenly made a lot more sense.
sentences.zh: 一陣冷風持續吹過山頂，強到讓 Sophia 之前叮嚀我們多穿幾層的話，突然變得非常有道理。

sentences.en: My face felt a little sunburned despite the cold, an odd combination Dad said happens often at this height, where the sun burns you even in freezing air.
sentences.zh: 儘管天氣很冷，我的臉還是有點曬傷，爸爸說在這個海拔常常會有這種奇怪的組合，即使空氣冷得要命，太陽還是會把你曬傷。

sentences.en: A small group of deer grazed calmly near the trailhead on our way back down, barely bothered by hikers passing just a few meters away.
sentences.zh: 下山的路上，靠近步道口的地方，一小群鹿正安靜地吃著草，幾乎不在意幾公尺外經過的登山客。

sentences.en: By the time we reached the car, my legs were sore enough that I had to rub my calves for several minutes just to feel normal again.
sentences.zh: 等我們回到車上，我的腿痠到得揉了好幾分鐘小腿，才總算感覺恢復正常。

sentences.en: William, meanwhile, fell asleep almost instantly, curled up sideways, still smelling faintly of cold mountain air and melted snow.
sentences.zh: 同時，William 幾乎是秒睡，整個人側躺蜷縮著，身上還隱約帶著冰冷山風和融雪的味道。

sentences.en: That night, Sophia gave us both a sour plum candy each, saying it always helps tired legs recover faster, a claim I have no way to prove or disprove.
sentences.zh: 那天晚上，Sophia 給了我們每人一顆酸梅糖，說這對疲累的腿恢復有幫助，這個說法我完全沒辦法證明或反駁。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: reaching a target this high in the cold and snow felt harder than almost anything else I have done this year, and somehow that made it better.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：在寒冷和雪地中抵達這麼高的一個目標，感覺比我今年做過的幾乎任何事都還難，而不知怎麼地，這反而讓一切變得更好。

quiz.question: What does Coco touch for the first time on this hike?
What does "source" mean in this chapter?
Why does Dad say Coco's face feels sunburned despite the cold air?
options: At high elevation, the sun can burn you even in freezing air.
She stood too close to a fire.
She fell asleep in direct sunlight for hours.
It is actually just windburn, not sunburn.

quiz.question: What marks the exact highest point of the trail?
options: A small wooden sign and a faded flag.
